title: A Prayer for a New Day
verse: This is the day the LORD has made; let us rejoice and be glad in it.
text:
Heavenly Father, thank You for the gift of this new day. As the sun rises, let Your light fill my heart with hope and purpose. I did nothing to earn this morning — the air in my lungs, the beat of my heart, the dawn breaking through my window — it is all pure grace from Your hand.

Guide my steps today, Lord. Order my path so that I walk where You want me to walk, speak what You want me to speak, and do what You want me to do. Guard my words — let nothing unwholesome come from my lips, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs.

Help me to be a blessing to everyone I meet today — the barista who makes my coffee, the colleague who frustrates me, the stranger who needs a kind word. Let me carry Your fragrance into every room I enter and leave every person a little more encouraged than I found them.

Give me strength for every challenge that lies ahead, patience in every trial that tests me, and a deep, unshakeable joy that comes not from my circumstances but from knowing that You, the Creator of the universe, are walking beside me every single moment. May everything I do today — the big achievements and the smallest tasks — bring glory to Your name.
closing: In Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Strength
verse: I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.
text:
Lord God, I come before You this morning asking for strength. Not the strength of the world — which is fleeting and unreliable — but Your divine strength, which is made perfect in my weakness. When I am at the end of my rope, that is where Your power begins.

I confess, Lord, that I often try to face life in my own strength. I push through exhaustion, muscle through problems, and pretend I have it all together. But this morning I lay down my self-sufficiency and pick up Your all-sufficiency. I cannot do this day alone, and You never asked me to.

Help me face this day with courage and faith. When I feel overwhelmed by my responsibilities, remind me that You are bigger than any problem, any deadline, any situation that feels impossible. When I feel alone — truly, deeply alone — remind me that You are Immanuel, God with me, and that You have promised never to leave me nor forsake me.

When my body is tired, renew my physical energy. When my mind is cluttered, bring clarity and focus. When my spirit is weary, pour out fresh oil. Fill me with Your Holy Spirit from the top of my head to the soles of my feet so I can live this day fully, freely, and fearlessly for You. I can do all things — not some things, not easy things — ALL things through Christ who strengthens me.
closing: In the mighty name of Jesus, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Guidance
verse: Trust in the LORD with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to Him, and He will make your paths straight.
text: Dear Lord, I don\
closing: In Your holy name I pray, Amen.

title: A Prayer of Gratitude
verse: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; His love endures forever.
text:
Father, before I ask for anything, I want to thank You. In a world that moves so fast, I want to slow down right now and simply say: thank You. Thank You for breath in my lungs — many did not wake up this morning, but I did, and that is not an accident. Thank You for a mind that can think, for eyes that can see, for a heart that can love and be loved.

Thank You for the people in my life — for the ones who encourage me, challenge me, and love me even when I\
closing: With a grateful heart, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Peace
verse: Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. I do not give to you as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.
text:
Prince of Peace, I bring You all my anxieties this morning. Lord, You know the thoughts that have been running circles in my mind — the worries that kept me awake, the fears about the future, the stress of all that lies ahead. My heart has been troubled, and my spirit has been restless. I lay every single one of them at Your feet right now.

The world offers a counterfeit peace — peace that depends on circumstances, peace that evaporates the moment things go wrong. But Your peace is different. It is supernatural. It doesn\
closing: I receive Your peace today, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Wisdom
verse: If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you.
text:
All-knowing God, I need Your wisdom today. The world is full of noise and opinions — social media feeds, news headlines, advice from well-meaning friends — but I want to hear Your voice above all others. Human wisdom is limited, biased, and constantly changing. But Your wisdom is pure, peaceable, gentle, and unchanging.

I face decisions today that require more than my own intelligence. Give me discernment to know what is true and what is false, what is essential and what is distraction, what is Your will and what is merely my desire. Help me to pause before reacting, to think before speaking, and to pray before deciding.

Grant me wisdom in my words — let me know when to speak up and when to remain silent, when to offer advice and when to simply listen. Grant me wisdom in my actions — help me to prioritize what truly matters and let go of what doesn\
closing: In the name of Jesus, who is our wisdom, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Courage
verse: Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the LORD your God will be with you wherever you go.
text: Mighty God, I confess that sometimes I feel afraid. Afraid of failure — that I\
closing: I go forward in Your strength, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Others
verse: Dear friends, let us love one another, for love comes from God. Everyone who loves has been born of God and knows God.
text: Loving Father, today I lift up the people around me — not just those I naturally gravitate toward, but every person You\
closing: May Your love flow through me today, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Faithfulness
verse: His mercies are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness.
text: Faithful God, Your mercies are new this morning, and I am overwhelmed with gratitude. Yesterday\
closing: I trust in Your faithfulness, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Purpose
verse: For we are God\
text: Creator God, You made me on purpose and for a purpose. Before I was formed in my mother\
closing: I walk in Your purpose today, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Humility
verse: He has shown you, O mortal, what is good. And what does the LORD require of you? To act justly and to love mercy and to walk humbly with your God.
text:
Lord, the world tells me to promote myself, to build my brand, to boast about my achievements, and to climb over others to get ahead. Social media rewards the loudest voices, the boldest claims, and the most polished image. But You call me to walk humbly — quietly, gently, with open hands instead of clenched fists.

Strip away my pride this morning, Lord. It hides in so many corners of my heart — pride disguised as confidence, pride dressed up as independence, pride masked as competence. Expose it all. Help me see myself clearly — not more than I am, which is arrogance, and not less than You made me to be, which is false humility.

Give me the grace to celebrate others without feeling threatened by their success. Give me the willingness to serve without needing recognition, applause, or a thank-you. Give me the maturity to admit when I\
closing: Teach me humility, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Patience
verse: Be completely humble and gentle; be patient, bearing with one another in love.
text: Patient Father, I confess that patience does not come naturally to me. I live in a world of instant everything — instant coffee, instant messages, instant answers. I want things now. I want results now. I want answers now. And when things don\
closing: Give me Your patience, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Kindness
verse: Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you.
text:
Gracious God, make me an instrument of Your kindness today. In a world that can be harsh, cynical, and cold, let me be a warm presence — a person who brings light into dark rooms and comfort into heavy hearts.

Help me to speak gently when I could speak harshly. Help me to listen carefully when I\
closing: Let Your kindness shine through me, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Work
verse: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord, not for human masters.
text:
Lord of all, I dedicate my work to You today. Whether my tasks feel exciting or mundane, groundbreaking or repetitive, meaningful or monotonous — help me to do them all with wholehearted excellence, as if I were doing them directly for You. Because ultimately, I am.

I confess that I sometimes see my work as just a paycheck, just a chore, just something I have to get through. But You see it differently. You are the God who works — You worked six days creating the heavens and the earth, and You call us to work as an act of worship. Whether I\
closing: I work for Your glory today, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Family
verse: As for me and my household, we will serve the LORD.
text:
Heavenly Father, I lift up my family to You this morning. You know each one intimately — their deepest joys, their hidden struggles, their unspoken fears, their secret needs. You know what makes them laugh, what keeps them awake at night, and what they are too proud to ask for help with. Love them in ways I cannot.

Bind us together with cords of love that cannot be broken by distance, disagreements, or the busyness that pulls us apart. In a world where families are under constant attack — from demanding schedules, financial pressure, digital distractions, and cultural confusion — be the anchor that holds us steady.

Heal any hurts that exist between us, Lord. Some wounds are fresh; others are scars from years ago that still ache when pressed. Where there is distance, bring closeness. Where there is unresolved conflict, bring the humility and courage needed for honest conversation. Where there has been betrayal, begin the long work of rebuilding trust. Where there is silence, fill it with words of love.

Protect each member of my family today — physically, emotionally, and spiritually. Guard their minds against lies and their hearts against temptation. Watch over the children; keep them safe from harm and rooted in faith. Strengthen the marriages; keep them passionate, faithful, and Christ-centered. Draw us all closer to You, and in drawing closer to You, draw us closer to each other.
closing: Bless my family, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Joy
verse: The joy of the LORD is your strength.
text:
God of joy, I choose joy today — not because everything is perfect, but because You are good. Not because life is easy, but because You are faithful.

Fill me with a joy that doesn\
closing: I choose Your joy today, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Self-Control
verse: Like a city whose walls are broken through is a person who lacks self-control.
text: Holy Spirit, I need Your help with self-control today. In a culture that glorifies indulgence, instant gratification, and the philosophy of \
closing: Produce Your fruit in me, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Forgiveness
verse: Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.
text: Merciful God, You have forgiven me of so much — more than I can comprehend. Every sin I\
closing: I choose to forgive, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Protection
verse: The LORD is my rock, my fortress and my deliverer; my God is my rock, in whom I take refuge.
text:
Mighty fortress, I take shelter in You this morning. Before I step outside my door and into the unpredictability of this day, I ask You to cover me with Your divine protection — a shield that no weapon, no scheme, and no attack can penetrate.

Shield me from physical harm today, Lord. Guard my steps, my travels, my comings and my goings. Whether I am driving, walking, working, or resting, be my ever-present protector. But I ask for more than physical safety.

Guard my mind from lies — the lies of the enemy that whisper I\
closing: I am safe in You, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Generosity
verse: Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over.
text:
Generous God, everything I have comes from You. Every dollar in my account, every hour in my day, every talent in my toolbox, every relationship in my life — they are all gifts from Your generous hand. I own nothing; I steward everything. Help me to hold it all with open hands instead of clenched fists.

Make me generous today, Lord — and not just with my money, though that matters too. Make me generous with my time — willing to stop my busy schedule to really listen to someone who needs to talk, willing to show up even when it\
closing: Make me generous like You, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Contentment
verse: I have learned the secret of being content in any and every situation.
text:
Lord, the world constantly tells me I need more — more money, more success, more followers, more stuff, a bigger house, a newer car, a better job, a different body. The advertisements never stop, the comparisons never end, and the goalpost keeps moving. But Your Word says godliness with contentment is great gain.

Teach me the secret Paul learned — to be content whether I have plenty or whether I have little. Not complacent, which settles for less than Your best. Not lazy, which refuses to grow. But genuinely content — at peace with where I am, grateful for what I have, and trusting You for what\
closing: You are my portion, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Lost
verse: The Lord is not slow in keeping his promise. He is patient with you, not wanting anyone to perish, but everyone to come to repentance.
text: Saving God, my heart breaks for those who don\
closing: Save the lost, Lord, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Integrity
verse: The integrity of the upright guides them, but the unfaithful are destroyed by their duplicity.
text:
Righteous God, help me to be the same person in private that I am in public. The same person at home that I am at church. The same person online that I am face to face. Help me to live with such consistent integrity that there is no gap between who I appear to be and who I really am.

In a world full of spin, half-truths, and carefully crafted images, integrity feels almost countercultural. Everyone curates their best self for the world to see while hiding their true self behind closed doors. But You call me to a different standard — a life of transparency, authenticity, and wholeness.

Let my word be my bond today, Lord. When I say I\
closing: Build integrity in me, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer Before Meals
verse: He gives food to every creature. His love endures forever.
text:
Provider God, thank You for this food before me. In a world where 828 million people go hungry every single day, I do not take this provision for granted. The fact that I have a meal in front of me is not simply due to my hard work or my planning — it is ultimately because of Your faithfulness and generosity.

Thank You for the unseen hands that made this meal possible — the farmers who planted and harvested, the workers who packaged and transported, the hands that prepared it. Behind every bite is a chain of provision that begins with You, the God who makes seeds grow and rain fall.

Bless this food to nourish my body and strengthen me for Your service. Let it fuel not just my muscles and my mind, but my mission — the work You\
closing: Thank You for Your provision, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Health
verse: Dear friend, I pray that you may enjoy good health and that all may go well with you, even as your soul is getting along well.
text: Great Healer, I thank You for my body — fearfully and wonderfully made by Your own hands. Every organ, every cell, every system working in concert is a testament to Your genius as Creator. I don\
closing: I trust You with my health, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Church
verse: For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them.
text: Head of the Church, I lift up Your bride today — the Church around the world. Pastors who preach faithfully week after week even when they\
closing: Revive Your Church, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Nations
verse: Ask me, and I will make the nations your inheritance, the ends of the earth your possession.
text:
King of Kings, I lift up the nations of the earth to You this morning. You love every people, every tribe, every tongue. You see every corner of this world — the bustling cities and the forgotten villages, the wealthy nations and the impoverished ones, the peaceful countries and the war-torn regions.

Bring peace where there is war, Lord. Right now, conflicts rage across this globe — soldiers fighting, families fleeing, children growing up knowing nothing but the sound of bombs. Intervene, Lord. Soften the hearts of the warmakers. Comfort the hearts of the victims. Bring leaders to the negotiating table and let justice and peace prevail.

Bring justice where there is oppression. There are nations where people cannot worship freely, where women are treated as property, where children are forced to work, where the powerful exploit the weak. You see it all, Lord, and it grieves Your heart. Raise up advocates, reformers, and voices of justice in every nation.

Raise up godly leaders who will govern with wisdom and righteousness rather than corruption and self-interest. Put people in positions of power who genuinely care about the welfare of their people and who will honor You in their decisions. And let the knowledge of Your glory cover the earth as the waters cover the sea — let there be no corner of this planet where Your name is not known and Your love is not felt.
closing: Your kingdom come on earth, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Children
verse: Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.
text:
Father of all, I pray for children everywhere this morning — for babies taking their first breaths, for toddlers discovering the world with wide eyes, for school-age children navigating friendships and learning, for teenagers struggling with identity and purpose. They are precious in Your sight, and every single one of them matters to You.

Protect them from harm, Lord. In a world that has become increasingly dangerous for the innocent, be their shield. Protect them from abuse — physical, emotional, and sexual. Protect them from predators who lurk online and in their communities. Protect them from the lies of this culture that tells them their worth is based on how they look, what they achieve, or how many likes they get.

Give them loving families and safe homes. Every child deserves to feel safe when they walk through their front door. Every child deserves to be told they are loved. For children in broken homes, be the stability they\
closing: Protect the children, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Surrender
verse: Not my will, but yours be done.
text:
Sovereign Lord, these were the words Jesus prayed in the Garden of Gethsemane on the night before He went to the cross — sweating drops of blood, overwhelmed with sorrow to the point of death, yet still choosing Your will over His own comfort. I make these sacred words my own this morning. Not my will, but Yours be done.

I surrender my agenda. I had plans for today — things I want to accomplish, places I want to go, outcomes I\
closing: Your will be done, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Hope
verse: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you trust in him, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
text: God of hope, some mornings the world feels incredibly heavy. The news cycle is relentless — war, disease, injustice, natural disasters, political chaos, economic uncertainty. The problems seem so massive, so impossible, so deeply rooted that hope itself feels foolish. And sometimes it\
closing: I overflow with hope, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for My Enemies
verse: But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.
text: Lord Jesus, this is one of the hardest things You ask of me — to pray for those who hurt me, misunderstand me, or work against me. My flesh wants revenge. My pride wants justice on my terms. My wounded heart wants them to feel the same pain they\
closing: I choose love over hate, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Discipline
verse: For the Spirit God gave us does not make us timid, but gives us power, love and self-discipline.
text:
Father, I confess that discipline does not come naturally to me. I am easily distracted — my phone buzzes and I lose an hour. I am quick to procrastinate — putting off hard things in favor of easy ones. I am prone to taking the path of least resistance, even when I know the harder path leads to better fruit. But You have given me a spirit of self-discipline, and I want to walk in it today.

Help me today to stay focused on what matters most. Not just what\
closing: I walk in discipline and purpose, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Lonely
verse: The LORD is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
text: Compassionate God, I lift up those who are lonely this morning — the elderly person who hasn\
closing: Use me to comfort the lonely, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Clean Hands and Pure Heart
verse: Who may ascend the mountain of the LORD? Who may stand in his holy place? The one who has clean hands and a pure heart.
text: Holy God, I want to walk before You today with absolute integrity. In a world that constantly tempts me to compromise, to cut corners, to look the other way, to click on things I shouldn\
closing: Create in me a clean heart, O God, Amen.

title: A Prayer for My Pastor
verse: Remember your leaders, who spoke the word of God to you. Consider the outcome of their way of life and imitate their faith.
text:
Lord, I lift up my pastor and church leaders to You this morning. They carry burdens I may never see — the weight of caring for hundreds of souls, each with their own struggles, questions, and crises. The pressure of preparing sermons week after week that are biblically faithful, practically relevant, and spiritually powerful. The challenge of leading imperfect people through imperfect situations with imperfect resources.

Refresh them today, Lord. Ministry is exhausting, and the demands are relentless. People call at all hours. Crises don\
closing: Strengthen my shepherd, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Creativity
verse: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text:
Creator God, You are the Author of all creativity — every color in the spectrum, every melody that moves the soul, every mathematical equation that holds the universe together, every sunset that paints the sky in colors no artist can replicate. Your imagination is infinite, boundless, and endlessly surprising. And You made me in Your image, which means there is a spark of creativity planted deep within me.

Unlock that creativity today, Lord. I don\
closing: Inspire me today, Creator God, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Financial Wisdom
verse: The blessing of the LORD brings wealth, without painful toil for it.
text: Provider God, I bring my finances before You this morning. You know every detail — my income and my expenses, my debts and my savings, my financial goals and my financial anxieties. Money is one of the most stressful areas of life, and I confess that I don\
closing: I trust You as my provider, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Missionaries
verse: Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
text:
Lord of the harvest, I pray for missionaries serving in difficult places around the world this morning — those who left their homes, their families, their comfort zones, their careers, and everything familiar to carry the gospel to people who have never heard it. They chose obedience over comfort, and their sacrifice is inspiring.

Protect them physically, Lord. Some serve in dangerous regions where persecution is real, where governments are hostile to the gospel, where their lives are literally at risk for simply speaking Your name. Surround them with Your angels. Shield them from harm. Give them supernatural discernment about when to advance and when to withdraw.

Protect them spiritually as well. The enemy targets missionaries with fierce intensity — loneliness, discouragement, culture shock, doubt, homesickness, marital strain, burnout. Strengthen their inner person. Refresh their passion for the work. Remind them daily why they said yes.

Provide for their needs, Lord. Many missionaries live on support that fluctuates with the economy and the faithfulness of donors. Meet their financial needs, their medical needs, their emotional needs. Sustain their families — their spouses who sacrificed alongside them and their children who are growing up far from extended family. Give them breakthroughs in the communities they serve. And stir up more workers for Your harvest, Lord — including me, whether it\
closing: Send more workers into the harvest, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Today\
verse: Let your conversation be always full of grace, seasoned with salt, so that you may know how to answer everyone.
text:
Lord, I will speak hundreds — maybe thousands — of words today. In meetings and in messages, at meals and in passing, through texts and emails and phone calls and face-to-face encounters. Each word has power to build up or tear down. Each sentence can be a gift or a weapon. Proverbs says the tongue has the power of life and death, and I want to choose life today.

Guard my tongue, Lord. Set a watch over my mouth. Before words leave my lips, filter them through three questions: Is it true? Is it kind? Is it necessary? Help me to eliminate careless words that wound, sarcastic jabs disguised as humor, and gossip that I excuse by calling it "prayer requests" or "concern."

Let my words be full of grace and truth today. Help me to listen more than I speak — really listen, not just wait for my turn to talk. Help me to encourage more than I complain, to affirm more than I criticize, to speak life into every person I encounter, especially those who rarely hear anything good about themselves.

When I\
closing: Season my words with grace, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Endurance
verse: Let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us, fixing our eyes on Jesus.
text:
Jesus, some mornings I feel like giving up — on a dream that seems to be dying, a relationship that takes more than it gives, a calling that costs more than I expected, or just the relentless grind of daily life. The race feels impossibly long, the finish line seems invisible, and my legs are shaking with exhaustion.

But You have marked out a specific race for me, and You\
closing: I will not give up, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Gratitude
verse: Give thanks in all circumstances; for this is God\
text: Father, before my complaints start, before my requests begin, before my worries take hold of this morning, I want to start with thanks. Pure, unfiltered, heartfelt gratitude. Thank You for the breath in my lungs — the miracle I take for granted with every inhale. Thank You for a brand new day that I didn\
closing: I am grateful, Lord, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Healing
verse: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
text: Healer of body and soul, I bring my pain to You this morning — every part of me that hurts, whether it\
closing: I believe in Your healing power, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Boldness
verse: For God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power and of love and of a sound mind.
text: Mighty God, I confess that fear often holds me back. Fear of failure — so I don\
closing: I walk in boldness and not fear, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Unity in My Home
verse: How good and pleasant it is when God\
text: Father, my home is where I am most real — where the mask comes off, where my best self and my worst self both show up, and where the people I love the most sometimes bear the brunt of my stress, exhaustion, and frustration. Arguments flare, misunderstandings multiply, and busy schedules pull us in different directions until we\
closing: Let peace rule in my home, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Oppressed
verse: Learn to do right; seek justice. Defend the oppressed. Take up the cause of the fatherless; plead the case of the widow.
text:
God of justice, I pray for those who are oppressed, marginalized, and voiceless around the world. Victims of human trafficking who are trapped in unspeakable conditions and have lost all hope of rescue. Communities living under tyranny where speaking out means imprisonment or death. The poor who are systematically exploited by unjust systems — paid unfairly, housed poorly, denied basic dignities.

You are not silent about injustice, Lord. All throughout Scripture, You champion the cause of the weak, the poor, the orphan, and the widow. You are a God who sees, who hears, and who acts. Injustice may appear to have the upper hand, but it never has the last word — You do.

Rise up and defend the defenseless, Lord. Break the chains of those who are enslaved. Expose the corrupt systems that profit from human suffering. Strengthen the organizations, advocates, and activists who are fighting for justice on the front lines. Give them resources, protection, and breakthroughs.

And use me as an instrument of Your justice, Lord. Open my eyes to the injustice around me — not just across the world, but in my own community. The person being bullied. The worker being exploited. The family being overlooked. Give me the courage to speak up when staying silent would be easier, to stand with the marginalized when it costs me something, and to use whatever influence I have to make the world more just.
closing: Let justice roll like a river, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer Before Reading Scripture
verse: Open my eyes that I may see wonderful things in your law.
text:
Holy Spirit, I am about to open Your Word. But before my eyes scan the page, before my fingers turn to the passage, I need You to open the eyes of my heart. Without You, these are just ancient words on a page. With You, they become living, active, sharper than any double-edged sword, able to penetrate the deepest parts of who I am.

Help me to see wonderful things I\
closing: Speak, Lord, Your servant is listening, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Neighbors
verse: Love your neighbor as yourself.
text:
Lord, You command me to love my neighbor — not just the ones I like, not just the ones who are easy to love, not just the ones who look and think and believe like me. Every person You have placed near me is my neighbor. The co-worker I struggle with. The person next door I barely know. The stranger I pass on the street without making eye contact. The difficult relative. The awkward acquaintance. All of them.

Give me Your eyes to see them, Lord. When I look at people, I often see inconveniences, interruptions, or strangers. But when You look at them, You see beloved children made in Your image, each one carrying their own story, their own pain, their own dreams. Help me to see people the way You do — as infinitely valuable and deeply loved.

Give me Your heart to love them. Not with distant, theoretical love that talks about compassion but never acts on it, but with hands-and-feet, roll-up-your-sleeves, show-up-at-the-door love. The kind that notices when someone is struggling and does something about it.

Show me one practical way I can be a good neighbor today — a kind word to the cashier who looks exhausted, a helping hand to the elderly neighbor with groceries, an invitation to coffee for the new person who doesn\
closing: Help me love my neighbor well, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Revival
verse: Will you not revive us again, that your people may rejoice in you?
text:
Lord God, we are in desperate need of revival — in our hearts, in our churches, in our communities, in our nations. We have drifted far from You, and the evidence is everywhere. Churches are emptying while anxiety is increasing. Morality is crumbling while entertainment is booming. We call evil good and good evil. We have exchanged Your truth for comfortable lies and Your presence for religious rituals.

But You are the God who revives dry bones and ignites cold hearts. You\
closing: Revive us again, O Lord, Amen.

title: A Prayer of Delight
verse: Delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
text: Gracious God, this morning I choose to delight in You — not in my circumstances, which change daily. Not in my achievements, which can be here today and forgotten tomorrow. Not in what I have or don\
closing: You are my delight, Lord, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Obedience
verse: If you love me, keep my commands.
text:
Lord Jesus, obedience is not popular in a world that celebrates autonomy, that glorifies doing whatever feels right, that worships personal freedom above all else. "Follow your heart" is the mantra of our culture. But You call me to a radically different standard — not blind obedience born of fear that says "I have to," but loving obedience born of relationship that says "I want to, because I love You."

Today, help me to obey You in the small things that no one sees — the private choices, the inner thoughts, the moments when I could get away with cutting corners. And help me to obey You in the big things that everyone notices — the public stands, the costly decisions, the moments that define my character.

When Your Word contradicts my desires, give me the strength to choose Your way over my feelings. Feelings change with the weather; Your Word stands forever. When the crowd goes one direction and You call me another, give me the courage to follow You alone, even if it means walking a lonely road.

My obedience is my love letter to You, Jesus. It\
closing: I choose obedience out of love, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Rest
verse: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.
text:
Dear Lord, the day is done and I come to You tired but grateful. Thank You for carrying me through every moment of this day — the moments that made me smile and the moments that made me sigh. The victories I celebrated and the battles that exhausted me. The conversations that encouraged me and the ones that drained me. You were in all of it, and I am still standing because of Your faithfulness.

As I lay down tonight, quiet my racing thoughts. My mind wants to replay the day — the mistakes I made, the things I should have said differently, the tasks I didn\
closing: I rest in You tonight, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Reflection
verse: Search me, God, and know my heart; test me and know my anxious thoughts.
text:
Heavenly Father, as this day ends, I invite You to search my heart with Your gentle, penetrating light. Not to condemn me, but to correct me. Not to shame me, but to shape me. Show me where I fell short today — where I chose selfishness over love, where I chose impatience over grace, where I chose fear over faith, where I chose comfort over obedience.

I think back through the hours of this day. The morning rush where I was too hurried to be kind. The midday stress where I snapped at someone who didn\
closing: I rest in Your forgiveness, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Protection
verse: He who watches over Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.
text:
Almighty God, as darkness falls outside my window, I trust in Your unfailing protection. The night can feel vulnerable — strange sounds amplified in the silence, shadows that seem to move, and the general unease that comes when the world goes dark. But You are the God who never sleeps, the guardian who never takes a break, the watchman who never abandons His post.

Post Your angels around my home tonight, Lord. Station them at every door, every window, every entry point. Protect my family from physical harm — from break-ins and fires, from accidents and illness, from anything that would threaten our safety while we are at our most vulnerable.

Protect my mind from anxiety and fear. The nighttime is when worries grow largest and darkest, when problems that seemed manageable during the day feel impossible in the silence. Guard my thoughts. Don\
closing: Under Your wings I find refuge, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Surrender
verse: Into your hands I commit my spirit; deliver me, LORD, my faithful God.
text:
Lord Jesus, these were the words You spoke from the cross — Your final breath spent in an act of total surrender to the Father. And they are my prayer tonight. I have nothing left to give from this day except my complete trust in You.

I surrender everything — my successes and my failures, my moments of faith and my moments of doubt, my hopes for the future and my fears about the unknown. I surrender the conversations that went well and the ones that went sideways. I surrender the things I accomplished and the things still sitting on my to-do list.

I cannot control tomorrow, Lord. I cannot fix everything that\
closing: Into Your hands I commit my spirit, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Loved Ones
verse: The LORD bless you and keep you; the LORD make His face shine on you and be gracious to you.
text: Caring Father, as the world grows quiet tonight, I lift up each person I love — one by one, face by face, name by name. You know them intimately, Lord. You know them even better than I do. You formed them in their mothers\
closing: I entrust them to Your care, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Thanksgiving
verse: Every good and perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of the heavenly lights.
text:
Generous God, before I close my eyes tonight, I want to count my blessings. Not because I have to, but because I want to — because gratitude is the best way to end a day. Every good thing in my life comes from You. Every kindness received came through Your orchestration. Every moment of unexpected laughter was a gift You planted in my day. Every provision met was Your faithfulness showing up again.

Thank You for today\
closing: With a thankful heart, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Tomorrow
verse: Do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
text: Lord, I confess that my mind has already raced ahead to tomorrow — to the alarm clock that will ring too early, to the tasks waiting on my desk, to the meetings on my calendar, to the problems that are still unsolved, to the unknowns that make my stomach tighten with anxiety. I\
closing: Tomorrow belongs to You, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Healing
verse: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
text:
Great Physician, I bring You the wounds of this day — every one of them. The harsh words that stung like a slap, replaying in my mind over and over. The disappointments that ached deep in my chest — the hope deferred that made my heart sick. The exhaustion that has settled deep in my bones, in my muscles, in the very marrow of who I am.

You are the healer of broken hearts and tired bodies. The God who tenderly binds up wounds with the care of a loving parent bandaging a child\
closing: I receive Your healing touch, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for God\
verse: Where can I go from your Spirit? Where can I flee from your presence?
text:
Ever-present God, sometimes the night feels long and lonely. The house is quiet, the world is asleep, and in the silence, I can feel the weight of my own solitude. But Your Word reminds me with absolute certainty that there is nowhere I can go where You are not already there. Not the highest height nor the deepest depth. Not the brightest day nor the darkest night.

You are with me in the light when everything is clear and easy and joyful. And You are equally with me in the darkness when the path is unclear, the struggle is real, and the joy has faded. You don\
closing: You are here, and that is enough. In Jesus\

title: The Lord\
verse: This, then, is how you should pray...
text:
Our Father in heaven, hallowed be Your name. Your name is holy, set apart, worthy of all reverence. In a world that uses Your name carelessly, I choose to honor it with awe and wonder tonight.

Your kingdom come, Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Let Your rule and reign extend into every corner of my life — my home, my work, my relationships, my heart. Whatever Your will is, Lord, let it override mine completely.

Give us today our daily bread. Thank You for providing everything I needed today — food, shelter, breath, strength. I trust You to do the same tomorrow. And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors. I receive Your forgiveness tonight, Lord, and I extend that same forgiveness to everyone who wronged me today. I release them. I set them free.

And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. Protect me from the traps the enemy has set. Keep me from the sin that so easily entangles. Deliver me from evil — external and internal, visible and invisible. For Yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever. Tonight and always, it all belongs to You.
closing: Amen.

title: A Prayer for Renewed Mind
verse: Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind.
text: Transforming God, as this day closes I ask You to renew my mind. Today the world poured a lot into my head — news headlines designed to frighten me, advertisements designed to make me feel inadequate, social media designed to make me compare, conversations that planted seeds of negativity. Some of what entered my mind today honored You, and some of it definitely didn\
closing: Renew my mind tonight, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Grace
verse: But he said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness."
text: God of all grace, I was not perfect today. Not even close. I said things I shouldn\
closing: I rest in Your grace, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Peaceful Sleep
verse: In peace I will lie down and sleep, for you alone, LORD, make me dwell in safety.
text: God of peace, I bring You my restless mind tonight. The thoughts that spiral outward like an unraveling thread — one worry connecting to another, then another, until the whole thing feels like an impossible knot. The to-do lists that grow longer every time I think about them, each item demanding attention I don\
closing: In peace I lie down, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Praise
verse: From the rising of the sun to the place where it sets, the name of the LORD is to be praised.
text: Worthy God, the sun has set on another day, and I want the last words on my lips before I sleep to be words of praise. Not because today was perfect — it wasn\
closing: All glory and honor to You, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Trust
verse: When I am afraid, I put my trust in you.
text: Trustworthy God, tonight I choose trust over worry. This is not a passive, easy choice — it\
closing: I trust You completely, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Perseverance
verse: Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest if we do not give up.
text:
Steadfast God, today was hard. Really hard. I felt like giving up — on my goals that seem to be taking forever, on my dreams that feel increasingly unrealistic, on that situation that never seems to change, on that person who never seems to hear me. The fight feels long, the progress feels painfully slow, and some days I wonder if any of it even matters.

But You remind me tonight: do not grow weary in doing good. The harvest is coming. It may not be visible yet — seeds don\
closing: I will not give up, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Purity
verse: Create in me a pure heart, O God, and renew a steadfast spirit within me.
text:
Holy God, as I end this day, I ask You to create a pure heart within me. Not repair the old one, but create something new — fresh, clean, unblemished by the stains of today. David prayed this prayer after his deepest failure, and I pray it tonight after mine.

Cleanse me from every impure thought that found a home in my mind today — the lustful glance I didn\
closing: Create in me a clean heart, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Weary
verse: He gives strength to the weary and increases the power of the weak.
text: Sustaining God, I am weary tonight. Weary in body — my muscles ache, my eyes are heavy, and my energy has been completely spent. Weary in spirit — the spiritual battles of life have drained reserves I didn\
closing: I lean on Your strength, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Gratitude
verse: In everything give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
text:
Good Father, before I sleep tonight, let me remember — let me really stop and remember — how good You have been today. Not in a rushed, obligatory way, but with genuine, slow, savoring gratitude.

Thank You for the morning light that woke me — golden and warm and full of promise. Thank You for the breath in my lungs all day long, the quiet miracle that I hardly ever think about. Thank You for food on my table — the taste, the nourishment, the simple pleasure of a good meal. Thank You for clean water to drink, a luxury that billions don\
closing: Thank You for everything, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Marriage and Relationships
verse: Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins.
text: God of love, I lift up my relationships to You tonight — the ones that bring me deepest joy and the ones that cause me the most pain. For those closest to me — my spouse, my family, my dearest friends — help us love each other better. Not the effortless love of good days, but the intentional, sacrificial, choosing-you-even-when-it\
closing: Strengthen our bonds of love, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Spiritual Growth
verse: But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ.
text: Lord Jesus, I don\
closing: Grow me in faith, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer Against Anxiety
verse: Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.
text: Caring Father, anxiety has been whispering to me all day — and now that the house is quiet and the lights are off, it\
closing: I cast my cares on You, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Direction
verse: Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light on my path.
text: Guiding God, I don\
closing: Light my path, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Brokenhearted
verse: The LORD is close to the brokenhearted and saves those who are crushed in spirit.
text: Compassionate God, my heart is heavy tonight. It feels like there\
closing: Be close to me tonight, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Unity
verse: How good and pleasant it is when God\
text:
God of peace, there is so much division in the world tonight. Nations are divided against nations, politicians against politicians, races against races, generations against generations. Even within the Church — Your own family — there is division over theology, worship styles, political affiliations, and social issues. It breaks Your heart, Lord, and tonight it breaks mine too.

Bring unity where there is division. Not uniformity — You created us different on purpose and our diversity is beautiful. But unity — the ability to disagree without dehumanizing, to hold different perspectives without holding grudges, to love each other fiercely even when we see things differently.

Bring understanding where there is judgment. Help me to stop assuming the worst about people who think differently than I do. Help me to listen before I label, to seek to understand before I seek to be understood. Bring love where there is hate. Replace contempt with compassion. Replace hostility with humility. Replace the need to be right with the willingness to be kind.

Start with me, Lord. I can\
closing: Make us one, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Eternal Perspective
verse: So we fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is unseen, since what is seen is temporary, but what is unseen is eternal.
text:
Eternal God, the troubles of today felt so big, so urgent, so all-consuming. They dominated my thoughts from morning until now. They demanded my attention, drained my energy, and made me feel like the weight of the world was on my shoulders. But tonight, as the world grows quiet and the noise fades, help me zoom out and see the bigger picture — Your picture.

This life is a breath, Lord. Beautiful and precious, but brief. A vapor that appears for a little while and then vanishes. Seventy or eighty years in the light of eternity is barely the blink of an eye. The things that stressed me today — the deadlines, the disagreements, the disappointments — most of them will be completely forgotten in a year, much less in a hundred years, much less in a million.

But some things done today will echo for eternity. The love I showed to that person who needed it. The kindness I offered to a stranger who was silently suffering. The faith I held onto when quitting would have been easier. The prayer I prayed for someone else\
closing: Fix my eyes on eternity, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Courage for Tomorrow
verse: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the LORD your God will be with you wherever you go.
text: Commander of heaven\
closing: I face tomorrow with courage, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Quiet Trust
verse: Be still, and know that I am God.
text:
Almighty God, I have done enough talking today. Enough doing. Enough striving. Enough managing, organizing, planning, answering, solving, performing, and worrying. I have spent this entire day in motion — physical, mental, emotional — and now I am spent. Now I simply want to be still.

Be still — two of the hardest words in the English language for a culture that glorifies hustle and productivity. We wear our busyness like badges of honor and feel guilty when we rest. But You don\
closing: I am still before You. Amen.

title: A Prayer for Wisdom in Relationships
verse: Above all, love each other deeply, because love covers over a multitude of sins.
text:
Father, as I lay down tonight I think of the people You have placed in my life — family by blood and family by choice, friends from childhood and friends from just last year, colleagues I see daily and neighbors I barely know. Some of these relationships bring deep joy and laughter and life. Others carry tension, confusion, hurt, and exhaustion. All of them matter to You.

Give me wisdom to love well, Lord. Loving people is the most important and most complicated thing I do, because every person is different and every relationship requires something unique from me. Help me to be patient when patience is the hardest thing in the world — when the same conversation happens for the hundredth time, when the same mistakes are repeated, when progress is measured in millimeters.

Help me to extend grace when I\
closing: I entrust my relationships to You tonight, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Praise Before Sleep
verse: Let everything that has breath praise the LORD. Praise the LORD.
text: Lord God, I don\
closing: All praise and glory to You, now and forever, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Contentment Tonight
verse: I have learned to be content whatever the circumstances.
text: Lord, as I close my eyes tonight, I confess that comparison and discontent have crept into my heart like uninvited guests. I scrolled through social media today and saw what others have — their beautiful homes, their seemingly perfect families, their career milestones, their exotic vacations, their picture-perfect lives — and a quiet voice whispered, "You\
closing: I am content in You, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Troubled Dreams
verse: When you lie down, you will not be afraid; when you lie down, your sleep will be sweet.
text: Father, sometimes my nights are haunted by anxious dreams, nightmares, and restless thoughts that replay the day\
closing: Grant me sweet and peaceful sleep, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for My Spouse
verse: Two are better than one, because they have a good return for their labor.
text: Lord, I lift up my partner to You tonight — this person You placed beside me, this imperfect, beautiful, frustrating, wonderful gift from Your hand. Marriage is hard, Lord. Nobody warned us it would be this hard. Or maybe they did, but we didn\
closing: Strengthen our marriage, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Single Season
verse: Delight yourself in the LORD, and he will give you the desires of your heart.
text: Father, tonight the silence of being single feels especially loud. The empty chair at the dinner table. The quiet apartment where no one asks about my day. The long evenings that stretch out with no one to share them. The couples I see everywhere — holding hands at the grocery store, laughing together at restaurants, posting anniversary photos online — each one a gentle reminder of what I don\
closing: You are my heart\

title: A Prayer for Those Grieving
verse: Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.
text:
Comforter God, tonight I pray for those who are grieving — and grief takes so many forms. There are those who lost a loved one recently — the wound is fresh, the shock is raw, and the absence feels like a physical ache in their chest. The empty side of the bed. The phone call they almost made before remembering. The place setting they unconsciously laid before catching themselves. Every room holds a memory. Every quiet moment invites a flood of tears.

There are those whose grief has lingered for years — the world has moved on but they haven\
closing: Comfort those who mourn tonight, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Repentance
verse: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.
text: Holy God, I come before You tonight with a heavy heart, weighed down by the baggage of my own failures. I sinned today. Maybe it was a harsh word that flew out of my mouth before my brain could catch it — a verbal grenade that detonated in someone\
closing: I receive Your forgiveness tonight, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Workers of the Night
verse: The LORD watches over you — the LORD is your shade at your right hand.
text: Lord, while I prepare for sleep — adjusting my pillow, pulling up my blankets, settling into the comfort of my bed — many are just starting their shifts. Nurses walking the sterile hallways of hospitals, checking on patients in the quiet hours when illness feels most frightening and loneliness most acute. Police officers patrolling dark streets, never knowing what the next call will bring, carrying the weight of a community\
closing: Protect the night workers, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Letting Go
verse: Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.
text: Father, I\
closing: I let go and trust You completely, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for My Parents
verse: Honor your father and your mother, so that you may live long in the land the LORD your God is giving you.
text:
Lord, I pray for my parents tonight — whether they are near or far, whether our relationship is close or complicated, whether they are living or have gone to be with You. Whatever the situation, they are part of my story, woven into the very fabric of who I am, and tonight I lift them before Your throne.

Thank You for the gift of life they gave me. Before I drew my first breath, they were already making sacrifices for me. Before I could speak my first word, they were already worrying about my future. They fed me when I was hungry, held me when I was scared, stayed up when I was sick, and cheered for me when I succeeded. They weren\
closing: Bless my parents tonight, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer Against Temptation
verse: No temptation has overtaken you except what is common to mankind. And God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear.
text: Faithful God, I faced temptation today — some I resisted, some I didn\
closing: Keep me from temptation, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for God\
verse: Your word is a lamp for my feet, a light on my path.
text: Lord, as I reflect on this day, I realize how much I need Your Word — not as an obligation to check off my spiritual to-do list, not as a religious duty performed out of guilt, but as a lifeline thrown to a drowning soul. Your Word is the lamp I need when the path ahead feels impossibly dark, when I can\
closing: Your Word is my light, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Persecuted Church
verse: Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
text: Lord Jesus, tonight I remember my brothers and sisters around the world who face persecution for their faith — and I am convicted by their courage. There are believers right now sitting in prison cells for the crime of owning a Bible — the same Bible I have a dozen copies of, the same Bible I sometimes leave gathering dust on my nightstand. There are Christians being beaten, tortured, and killed for attending church — the same church attendance I sometimes skip because I\
closing: Strengthen the persecuted church, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Tomorrow\
verse: If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you.
text: Wise God, tomorrow I will face decisions — some so small I won\
closing: Guide my decisions, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer of Childlike Faith
verse: Truly I tell you, unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
text: Papa God, I\
closing: I trust You like a child trusts their father, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Unfulfilled Promises
verse: For no matter how many promises God has made, they are "Yes" in Christ.
text: Faithful God, there are promises You\
closing: I trust Your promises, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for the Addicted
verse: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
text:
Deliverer God, tonight I pray for those trapped in the suffocating grip of addiction — alcohol that promises relief but delivers destruction. Drugs that offer a temporary escape from pain but create a permanent prison of dependence. Pornography that counterfeits intimacy while poisoning the capacity for real connection. Gambling that dangles hope while stealing everything of value. Food, screens, shopping, work — even good things twisted into chains when they become the thing we cannot live without.

They feel powerless tonight, Lord. Powerless against the cravings that hijack their brain chemistry and override their willpower. Powerless against the shame that follows every relapse, the shame that whispers "you\
closing: Break every chain, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Eternal Perspective
verse: For our light and momentary troubles are achieving for us an eternal glory that far outweighs them all.
text: Eternal God, today\
closing: I fix my eyes on eternity, in Jesus\

title: A Prayer for Unspoken Needs
verse: Before they call I will answer; while they are still speaking I will hear.
text: All-knowing God, there are needs in my heart tonight that I can\
closing: You know my heart, Lord. I rest in that tonight, Amen.

title: A Prayer of Final Surrender
verse: Into your hands I commit my spirit; deliver me, LORD, my faithful God.
text:
My faithful God, this day is done. Completely, irreversibly, finally done. I cannot add one more minute to it, cannot undo a single moment, cannot replay a single conversation with different words. Whatever was accomplished today — the victories, the breakthroughs, the small kindnesses, the quiet moments of faithfulness — I offer them to You as imperfect gifts from imperfect hands. Whatever was left undone — the unfinished tasks, the unanswered emails, the unchecked boxes, the goals that remain just out of reach — I release them into Your hands without guilt, without shame, without the nagging feeling that I should have done more.

Into Your hands I commit my spirit tonight. These are the same hands that formed Adam from dust and breathed him to life. The same hands that parted the Red Sea and carved commandments in stone. The same hands that were pierced with nails on a Friday and raised in triumph on a Sunday. There are no safer hands in all of existence, and tonight I place everything I am and everything I have into them.

My worries — into Your hands. My hopes — into Your hands. My loved ones — into Your hands. My tomorrow with all its unknowns — into Your hands. My very life, every breath of it, from this moment until my last — into Your hands. You are faithful. You have always been faithful. You will always be faithful. You are good — good when I can see it and good when I can\
closing: Into Your hands I commit everything, Amen.

title: A Prayer for Strength to Forgive
verse: Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.
text: Merciful Father, tonight there is someone I need to forgive — and honestly, I don\
closing: I choose to forgive as You forgave me, in Jesus\
